title: Webhook URL returns 404 error
symptom: When testing a form trigger, the popup opens a wrong URL and returns a 404 error.
root_cause: The 404 error occurs because the webhook URL is incorrectly configured, likely missing the 'https://' prefix or not matching the expected service URL. This can happen if environment variables are not set correctly in the Docker Compose file.
fix_steps: Update the environment variables in your Docker Compose file to include the correct SERVICE_FQDN_N8N and SERVICE_URL_N8N values.
Ensure that the N8N_EDITOR_BASE_URL and WEBHOOK_URL are set to the correct SERVICE_URL_N8N.
Restart the n8n service to apply the changes.
agent_instruction: Update the Docker Compose environment variables and restart the n8n service.
